briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon/evening. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 21, 2026. We will now begin.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Escalation and the Urgency of Response
sections.content: Currently, the global climate crisis is intensifying, underscoring the urgent need for immediate action. Specifically, due to this year's 'Super El Niño' phenomenon, South Korea is concerned about heatwaves, torrential rains, and food supply shortages [5], with climate change already impacting food security [1]. In Western Europe and the UK, record-breaking heatwaves exceeding 40 degrees Celsius have led to emergency situations, including the early closure of the Eiffel Tower and school suspensions [18, 30], and the recurrence of the El Niño phenomenon is raising concerns about crop failures, similar to past drought cases in Ethiopia [31]. These climate changes are significantly impacting 40,000 regions and 5,100 species, hitting wildlife hardest in unexpected places [20, 21]. Furthermore, the spread of weather-related misinformation, such as 'rain all month long' [19], further highlights the importance of accurate information dissemination.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Efforts Towards Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating. Gwangmyeong City has demonstrated its competitiveness as a leading carbon-neutral city by winning the 'Minister's Award from the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment' through its green building policies [6, 8, 10]. In local communities, carbon neutrality initiatives are active, including Gyeyang-gu's environmental protection campaign [11] and children's experiential activities by the Birae-dong Carbon Neutrality Practice Alliance [9]. In the energy transition sector, the Ministry of Environment plans to produce eco-friendly hydrogen using small-scale hydropower, a renewable energy source, for the first time in Korea [14], and investments in high-value new industries such as advanced composite materials and new and renewable energy are also expanding [15]. The 9th popularly elected Yeosu City and Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province are also accelerating the establishment of energy transition strategies and the development of spaces for carbon neutrality practices [12, 13].

sections.title: 🌍 Societal and Ecosystem Changes Caused by Climate Change and Response Discussions
sections.content: Climate change is causing complex transformations across our society and ecosystems, beyond mere temperature increases. At the COP31 conference, the World Health Organization (WHO) emphasized that human health must be central to climate change discussions [4], and suggested that climate change factors are present in Europe's heatwaves [22]. Climate change is unevenly affecting air pollution in Taiwan [26] and causing seasonal shifts, such as rewriting the rules of the winter solstice [27]. Research has also shown that while white roofs and parks in cities have a cooling effect, they are insufficient to offset extreme global warming [25]. These changes are directly impacting economic sectors such as the insurance industry [2], making it urgent to explore multi-faceted response strategies.

sections.title: 🌱 Innovative Approaches for Climate Adaptation and a Sustainable Future
sections.content: Amidst the threats of climate change, innovative approaches and investments for adaptation and solutions are continuing. Lotte Mart is enhancing the resilience of agricultural production by selling Daegwallyeong spinach and smart farm strawberries to adapt to climate change [3]. At the corporate level, Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance is strengthening its ESG management by expanding carbon neutrality, green finance, and win-win management [7], and there has also been a suggestion that Samsung Electronics should learn from Taiwan's TSMC's renewable energy strategy [16]. Internationally, positive transition examples are emerging, such as most of Kenya's power grid operating on renewable energy, and Nairobi entrepreneurs illuminating Africa's future with standalone solar power [17]. These efforts demonstrate that there is still time for climate change and global warming, and that solutions can be found [29].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for June 21, 2026. We look forward to providing more detailed updates in our next briefing.

articles.title: Eiffel Tower Closes Early Due to Heatwave Hitting Western Europe... World Cup Street Cheering Canceled in Spain

articles.description: As a severe heatwave sweeps across Western Europe, countries are implementing safety precautions. France, anticipating temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius to persist, will close numerous schools on the 22nd (local time). The Minister of Education, Édouard Geffray, indicated that many elementary schools in regions under a red heatwave alert on the 22nd would be affected.

summary.phenomenon: On the 20th (local time), a heatwave exceeding 40 degrees Celsius swept across Western Europe, leading to the early closure of the Eiffel Tower in Paris, France, and the cancellation of World Cup street cheering in Spain, as countries implemented safety precautions. In France, many schools in areas under a red heatwave alert are scheduled to close on the 22nd (local time).
summary.outlook: In France, a heatwave exceeding 40 degrees Celsius is expected to continue for some time.

articles.original_title: “올여름 한 달 내내 비” “45도 폭염 30일 이상”?···여름마다 도는 가짜뉴스에 속지 않으려면
articles.description: Last month, fake news regarding the rainy season and heatwaves circulated on social media platforms like Threads and Instagram. Recurring weather-related misinformation, such as predictions of 'heavy rain throughout the 32-day rainy season this summer' or 'temperatures exceeding 45 degrees Celsius for over 30 days,' is spreading again this year. The Korea Meteorological Administration has clarified that these predictions are unfounded.

summary.phenomenon: Last month, weather-related fake news, such as 'heavy rain throughout the 32-day rainy season this summer' and 'temperatures exceeding 45 degrees Celsius for over 30 days,' spread on social media platforms like Threads and Instagram.
summary.outlook: The Korea Meteorological Administration stated that all these predictions are unfounded.

articles.original_title: Record-breaking heat expected across UK this week, says Met Office
articles.description: Health alerts are in place as very high humidity adds to danger of heat stress for the most vulnerableThe Met Office has expanded its extreme heat warning for the UK, predicting record-breaking highs of 38C (100.4F) this week.The Met Office forecasts that extremely high temperatures could last from Monday until Thursday, leading to health concerns for elderly and vulnerable people. The forecaster said there was “growing confidence” that this week may break the record for the hottest June tempera

summary.phenomenon: The UK Met Office has announced that record-breaking highs of 38°C (100.4°F) are expected across the UK from Monday to Thursday this week, with a high probability of breaking the June temperature record. Additionally, very high humidity is exacerbating the risk of heat stress for vulnerable populations.
summary.outlook: This heatwave could cause health problems for the elderly and vulnerable groups, and the UK Met Office has issued health alerts for extreme heat.

articles.title: El Niño is back with a vengeance – and fears of ‘Godzilla’ strength may be the least of our worries
articles.description: UN’s Word Food Programme and agriculture agency issue joint appeal for funds to avert global hunger crisis before it happensAdugna Woyessa was a little boy the first time drought tore his country apart. As harvests failed in rain-starved regions of Ethiopia in the early 1970s, and his school turned a classroom into a grain store for farmers to send aid, he had no idea that scientists were beginning to connect the force parching its fields with cyclical shifts in trade winds that had long superch

summary.phenomenon: The El Niño phenomenon has re-emerged. In the early 1970s, drought led to harvest failures in rain-deficient regions of Ethiopia.
summary.cause: Scientists began to link the force causing drought to cyclical shifts in trade winds.
summary.outlook: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) and the agriculture agency have issued a joint appeal for funds to avert a global hunger crisis before it occurs.
